intro: Desmochaeta muricata, also known as the "milk flower" or "milk vine," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique, small white flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating gastrointestinal issues, and exerting antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Desmochaeta muricata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive problems and infections.
